title: Dense Pressure-Cooked Malaysian Celery Stew
description: Sour, fluffy, meal-prep Malaysian dish featuring celery, pressure-cooked, ayurvedic-sattvic-friendly, served in a clay cazuela. Perfect for a weeknight dinner or meal prep for the next day.
instructions: Heat the coconut oil in a pressure cooker over medium heat.
Add the sliced ginger and cook, stirring occasionally, until fragrant, about 3 minutes.
Add the sliced lemongrass, chili peppers, and cook, stirring occasionally, for an additional 2 minutes.
Add the chopped celery, tamarind paste, fish sauce, salt, and black pepper. Stir to combine.
Add the coconut water and bring to a boil.
Lock the pressure cooker lid and cook at high pressure for 10 minutes.
Let the pressure release naturally for 5 minutes, then quick-release any remaining pressure.
Stir in the chopped cilantro and serve hot, garnished with additional cilantro if desired.
Refrigerate or freeze for meal prep.
Reheat as needed, garnished with additional cilantro if desired.
